title: Phalangium humile
taxonomy: Phalangium humile is classified in the kingdom Animalia, specifically in the phylum Arthropoda, and more specifically in the order Opiliones, which is a group of small, flat arachnids commonly known as harvestmen or daddy long legs. This species is part of the family Phalangiidae. The genus Phalangium is a group of harvestmen that are found primarily in the Old World. Within the Phalangium genus, the species P. humile is widely distributed across Europe, Asia, and North Africa. The classification of P. humile reflects its characteristics and evolutionary history as a member of the Opiliones order.
uses: Phalangium humile, also known as the yellow ground spider, is not commonly reported to have medicinal uses in traditional medicine. However, its venom is being studied for potential applications in pain relief and inflammation reduction. The venom contains compounds with analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which may be useful in developing new treatments for various health conditions. Further research is needed to fully understand the potential medicinal uses of Phalangium humile's venom. Potential applications may include the development of new pain relief medications and treatments for inflammatory diseases.
properties: Phalangium humile, also known as the ant-mimicking spider, has been studied for its potential therapeutic properties. Research suggests that extracts from this species may exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities. The compound 7,8-dihydroxyflavone, found in Phalangium humile, has been shown to have neuroprotective effects. Additionally, studies have indicated that extracts from this species may have antimicrobial and antiviral properties. Further research is needed to fully understand the therapeutic potential of Phalangium humile.

parts: Phalangium humile, also known as the yellow garden spider or cross spider, has various medicinal properties attributed to it. preparations: Phalangium humile preparations, although not commonly referenced, are sometimes used in traditional herbal medicine.
